% Generated by following the `create-beamer` + `beamer-authoring` skills.
% Portable default look: compiles with lualatex or pdflatex, no fontspec.
\documentclass[aspectratio=169]{beamer}
\usepackage{lmodern}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{tikz}
\usetikzlibrary{calc}

\definecolor{obAccent}{HTML}{1A365D}
\usecolortheme{default}
\setbeamertemplate{navigation symbols}{}
\setbeamercolor{frametitle}{fg=obAccent}
\setbeamercolor{title}{fg=obAccent}
\setbeamercolor{structure}{fg=obAccent}
\setbeamerfont{frametitle}{series=\bfseries}
\setbeamertemplate{frametitle}{%
	\vspace{4mm}\insertframetitle\par {\color{obAccent}\rule{\textwidth}{1pt}} %
}

\title{open-beamer}
\subtitle{AI-authored Beamer with a live preview}
\author{Demo}
\date{2026}

\begin{document}
	\begin{frame}[plain]
		\titlepage

		\begin{enumerate}
			\item Primeiro

			\item Segundo

			\item Terceiro
		\end{enumerate}
	\end{frame}

	\begin{frame}{Agenda}
		\begin{itemize}
			\item The problem with authoring Beamer

			\item How open-beamer works

			\item What you get today
		\end{itemize}
	\end{frame}

	\begin{frame}{LaTeX gives great output, a rough edit loop}
		\begin{columns}[T, onlytextwidth]
			\column{0.5\textwidth}
			\textbf{Strengths}
			\begin{itemize}
				\item Typography and math

				\item Consistency and version control
			\end{itemize}
			\column{0.46\textwidth}
			\textbf{Pain}
			\begin{itemize}
				\item Write, compile, squint, repeat

				\item No visual feedback

				\item Fine layout fights the engine
			\end{itemize}
		\end{columns}
		\vspace{4mm}
		\begin{block}{Takeaway}
			The output is worth it; the edit loop is what hurts.
		\end{block}
	\end{frame}

	\begin{frame}{How it works}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
			\fill[obAccent]
				($(current page.north east)+(-2.2cm,-0.9cm)$)
				circle
				(1.4mm);
		\end{tikzpicture}
		\begin{enumerate}
			\item The \texttt{.tex} stays the source of truth.

			\item A dev server compiles each deck to PDF on save.

			\item The browser renders the PDF and hot-reloads.

			\item An AI skill drafts the deck from a prompt.
		\end{enumerate}
	\end{frame}

	\begin{frame}[plain]
		\centering
		\vfill
		{\Huge\color{obAccent}\textbf{2 passes}}\par
		\vspace{3mm}
		{\large compiled per save, so \texttt{remember picture} overlays land right}
		\vfill
	\end{frame}

	\begin{frame}{What you get today}
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tabular}{@{}ll@{}}
				\toprule \textbf{Capability}     & \textbf{Status} \\
				\midrule Live PDF preview        & ready           \\
				Page navigation and present mode & ready           \\
				Hot-reload on save               & ready           \\
				AI deck generation               & this skill      \\
				\bottomrule
			\end{tabular}
		\end{center}
		\vspace{3mm}
		Ranges like weeks 1--2 use an en-dash, never a long dash.
	\end{frame}

	\begin{frame}[plain]
		\centering
		\vfill
		{\Huge\color{obAccent}\textbf{Thank you}}\par
		\vspace{4mm}
		{\large github.com/NicolasArthurDev/open-beamer}
		\vfill
	\end{frame}
\end{document}
